claim_status: Contained M0 start-state inventory only; no proposed task approval, no TASK-000011 acceptance, no readiness promotion, no broad M1 implementation claim, no developer preview claim, no beta claim, no stable claim, no production claim, no Chrome-class claim, no performance claim, no compatibility claim, no security claim, no accessibility claim, no release readiness claim, no daily-driver claim, and no all-information-ready-for-building claim.

allowed_actions: Continue documentation, research, registry synchronization, validators, no-claim evidence captures, and task-approval preparation that preserve current gate truth.
Run validation, fix stale links or drift, and add no-claim handoff artifacts when they make the stop/resume path clearer.
required_before_start: Map the work to a current PB, RQ, WP, TASK, requirement, risk, ADR, or documented handoff lane before editing.
Keep unsupported behavior, owner-only decisions, and no-claim boundaries beside every new evidence statement.
must_stop_before: Stop before treating no-claim research, a proposed task row, or a template as execution approval or readiness promotion.
Stop before changing product, security, compatibility, accessibility, performance, Chrome-class, release, production, or daily-driver claims.

allowed_actions: Maintain the TASK-000011 review handoff, non-accepting evidence capture, source-commit binding, and validation links while the task remains review_pending.
Prepare independent-review inputs without changing task status, accepting the task, or promoting PB-011.
required_before_start: Verify TASK-000011 remains review_pending and that any evidence capture records needs_independent_review unless an independent reviewer creates a separate accepted bundle.
Keep TASK-000011 scope limited to the contained M0 WP-002 reference and documented limitations.
must_stop_before: Stop before changing TASK-000011 to accepted, complete, or release-gated without an independent accepted evidence bundle.
Stop before claiming owner-reviewed IPC readiness, renderer security, agent security, process isolation, site isolation, production IPC, or implementation readiness.
prohibited_claims: No production IPC or security claim.

allowed_actions: Prepare owner-review material, task manifests, evidence-bundle targets, and scope clarification for proposed TASK-000001 through TASK-000010.
Update the task queue, dependency graph, and readiness docs when a proposed task's preconditions, allowed paths, or evidence requirements drift.
required_before_start: A proposed task must become an immutable reviewed task manifest with owner approval, independent reviewer assignment, allowed paths, prohibited paths, resource budget, rollback plan, expiry, and evidence bundle location.
The checked no-claim task approval template must be replaced with task-specific reviewed evidence before execution starts.
must_stop_before: Stop before implementing TASK-000001 through TASK-000010 from the proposed queue alone.
Stop before treating task queue presence, dependency-graph edges, or templates as owner-approved execution authority or a task approval claim.

allowed_actions: Continue no-claim research, planning, validation, and owner-decision packet preparation for broad implementation blockers.
Keep Chrome-class and extreme-performance goals traced to evidence requirements without representing them as current capability.
required_before_start: Broad M1 or product implementation requires owner-reviewed closure of source strategy, fresh-host, IPC, sandbox, benchmark, native-shell, profile/session, package/update, incident-response, backup-ownership, build-readiness closure, and release-authority gates.
Chrome-class, speed, memory, energy, compatibility, security, accessibility, production, release, or daily-driver claims require retained raw evidence and owner-reviewed claim bundles.
must_stop_before: Stop before starting broad browser construction, developer preview, beta, stable, production, or Chrome-class implementation from current contained M0 evidence.
Stop before importing release-path browser engine code, selecting a release UI toolkit, or publishing performance and security claims.
prohibited_claims: No performance, compatibility, security, or accessibility claim.
